title: AI Engineer — Big Tech
setting: Big tech company (Google, Meta, Amazon, Microsoft)
description: You're an AI Engineer at a major tech company. You work on models and systems that serve millions of users. The scale is massive, the tooling is world-class, and the problems are complex. You're surrounded by brilliant people, and your work ships to production at a scale most engineers never experience.

schedule.activity: Check overnight training runs — review loss curves, check for divergence, compare against baseline
schedule.note: Training often runs overnight on GPU clusters

schedule.activity: Team standup — each person shares experiment results, blockers, and priorities
Deep work: implement a new attention mechanism variant and set up ablation studies
Lunch with the NLP team — discuss a paper from last week's arXiv digest
Design review — present your model architecture changes to the team for feedback
Code review — review a colleague's feature engineering pipeline PR
Debug a production inference latency issue — profile the model serving pipeline
Write an experiment doc for next week's A/B test comparing model versions
Kick off a new training run with updated hyperparameters, set up monitoring alerts
Read 1-2 papers related to current project, take notes for team discussion

responsibilities: Design and train ML models at scale
Run experiments and publish internal results
Optimise model performance and inference latency
Participate in code and design reviews
Collaborate with product and infra teams
challenges: Slow iteration cycles (training can take days/weeks)
Navigating large codebases and internal tools
Keeping up with the pace of research
Balancing research exploration with product deadlines
rewards: Work on problems at unprecedented scale
Learn from the best engineers in the world
Your work impacts millions of users
